name: Prophet Muhammad
shortDescription: Prophet Muhammad is considered the final prophet in Islam, believed to have received divine revelations from Allah through the angel Jibreel (Gabriel). He is revered as the founder of Islam and the restorer of the original monotheistic faith of Adam, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus.
description: Prophet Muhammad, born in Mecca around 570 CE, is the central figure of Islam and widely regarded as the last prophet sent by God (Allah). According to Islamic belief, he received revelations from Allah through the angel Jibreel (Gabriel) starting around the age of 40. These revelations, recorded in the Quran, form the basis of Islamic scripture. Muhammad's life and teachings, as documented in the Hadith (sayings and actions), provide a model for Muslims to follow. His journey, known as the Hijra, from Mecca to Medina in 622 CE marks the beginning of the Islamic calendar and a turning point in the establishment of the Muslim community. He unified most of Arabia under a single religious and political system by the time of his death in 632 CE. Muslims believe that Muhammad restored the original, uncorrupted monotheistic faith of earlier prophets like Abraham, Moses, and Jesus. While not considered divine himself, he is revered as the perfect example of human conduct and submission to God's will. The Shahada, the Islamic declaration of faith, affirms that "There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is his messenger."
symbols: Sword (Zulfiqar, though more associated with Ali)

sources.title: Muhammad: A Prophet for Our Time
sources.author: Karen Armstrong
